data.epi_content:
"Vivian, why did you come all the way to the classroom when you just saw me last night?"

"I haven't seen you around the restaurant district lately, so I came to invite you to have a meal together. But I'm not sure what you two were doing with Evan putting that necklace around your neck."

Does Vivian think Evan put the necklace around my neck? The way she's frowning while speaking is quite something.

How can she look so beautiful even with such an expression? Whether it's her face, her aura, her development, or her abilities—I don't have anything better than Vivian.

"Well, I'm not sure myself. Evan just showed up unexpectedly. Evan, why did you come here again?"

When I asked with a deliberately expressionless face as if nothing had happened, Evan's face hardened as he spoke to Vivian.

"Because you were being bullied, so I came to help."

Vivian asked from the side, "Bullied?" but I ignored her and continued my conversation with Evan.

"Why now?"

"Because I was waiting for you to ask for help. Or at least tell Vivian about it. If I had stepped in to help you first... your pride would be hurt, and you'd be acting just like this."

"I see. That's right. That's how I was."

So Evan was trying to be considerate in his own way.

Besides, with Vivian standing next to us, his speech is quite gentlemanly.

Lydia probably won't matter.

She might enjoy secretly tormenting me from behind, but she wouldn't dare bully me openly in front of Vivian and Evan.

That girl is just a pathetic, low-class coward.

That's why I tried to endure it somehow, despite how difficult it was.

"Hey Vivian, did you know? Evan and I were once engaged. We almost got married. Our parents met, and fortunately for Evan, it was only discussed verbally."

But now, I've lost the emotional capacity to appreciate someone's consideration and respond in kind.

Just like when Evan came to find me before and killed me with his words, my mind has calmed at the thought of not having to hear such harsh words again.

I wonder if Vivian has caught on to what I'm about to say.

She makes a slightly dark expression, looking weary.

"But then my family collapsed, and Evan prefers you over me.

And I'm someone with nothing, even lacking in ability. Ahaha.

Let's leave it at that."

I wonder if Evan could be considered my ex-fiancé? Well, he probably wouldn't think so.

It was a promise made when we were about five years old, with the elders of our families casually suggesting it.

We kept meeting and playing together, and until I came to the Academy, I thought Evan was the only one for me.

If my family had remained intact, Evan wouldn't have been able to dismiss our childhood promise so easily, no matter how much he liked Vivian.

But I'm someone who offers no benefit in marriage.

So "what if" is just "what if."

No matter what hypothetical scenario I imagine, my current situation won't change.

It feels like whenever I try to do something, everything comes forward to deny me.

I was just planning to live humiliatingly and submissively under Lydia, graduate, and then hide away somewhere quiet and uninhabited.

It was just a hopeful, empty thought.

Still, my intention to live with will and hope hasn't changed.

"How about we just say we were playing a hot lovers' game with the necklace?"

"Does Erica want to just brush this off so lightly?"
data.epi_content2:
"Yes, I'm tired, Vivian. Can't you just take Evan and go get something to eat?"

"You don't even realize how you look right now."

"How? How do I look?"

I'm too annoyed to use formal speech anymore.

Since using informal speech doesn't feel that uncomfortable, I'll just speak rudely like a villain.

The resentment that had built up in my heart didn't burst; instead, it felt like it had sunk down to my heart.

"Why, how do I look right now? Like an idiot, I know.

How do you see me?"

Despite my sharp tone, Vivian doesn't avoid my gaze and answers confidently.

And even that aspect of her is so beautiful that the jealousy I've always felt in my heart grows stronger.

"Your eyes are sunken as if you haven't been sleeping properly, and your once vibrant white hair now looks dry and brittle.

Your already pale skin has become even paler, to the point where you could be mistaken for a corpse, and your gaze seems unfocused, as if you're staring into empty space."

"Yes, I look pathetic. I'm sorry. Compared to you, I'm quite a mess."

"That's not what I meant!"

"Then what did you mean?"

"Erica, you need to rest, whether it's your mind or body, you need to be at ease..."

"If that were possible, would I be acting like this?!"

I slammed the desk with all my strength.

I thought the wounds on my arm had all healed thanks to the healing magic Evan had used earlier.

But it seems they were only superficially closed, as they reopened and started bleeding again.

Anyway, when I shouted, Vivian was about to speak but then closed her mouth.

Looking into her eyes for a long time without breaking eye contact, I could see she was overlapping me with someone else.

Probably her father.

The difference is that Vivian's father could ascend to heaven, while I couldn't.

I look at Evan, who appears unfazed but whose right hand is clenched into a trembling fist.

Then I closed my eyes briefly before opening them again.

"Hey, Evan. What do you think about stepping backward into the past for a hopeful life? For a better future?"

"Stop saying weird things.

What's wrong with you?"

The burnt necklace is bothering me.

I tried to tear it off forcefully, but I just ended up choking myself, so I found the clasp at the back and undid it.

"Why, aren't there novels like that in the marketplace yet?

Like waking up after dying to find yourself younger, or getting to restart your life."

When I mentioned dying, Evan exclaimed in horror.

"That's absurd..."

"Yes, it's an absurd story.

An absurd story."

How can it make sense that you don't die even when you die?

This might actually be a dream.
data.epi_content3:
And I might be repeating this thought over and over again.

"But I realized that even if I go back to the past, there's no answer for me."

I just kept dying repeatedly.

"It feels like I'm a person who was created specifically to die."

Before Lydia put the dog collar on me, I thought deeply.

Rather than self-destructive thoughts like "why is this happening to me," I tried hard to recall how my family collapsed.

And I realized that the Mecklenburg family was completely evil, full of villains.

If you ask what evil deeds I did at the Academy, there's nothing except slapping Vivian.

And although I didn't directly participate in what my family did, nor did I openly defend it, I did live a prosperous and relatively happy life until it collapsed.

My brother, who was branded a traitor and killed, was extremely capable.

And somewhat cruel too.

It was my brother who suggested to my father that slaves who couldn't meet their quotas for picking fruits or cotton should have their wrists or ankles cut off rather than being killed.

I just remembered now; I had pushed these unfavorable memories to the back of my mind.

He was probably meant to be resurrected as some kind of zombie and appear as a villain later in the story.

But I never actually saw him appear.

Maybe the author wrote it and then forgot about it.

Who knows, he might have appeared near the end, caused trouble, and then been dealt with.

"I think I know why my brother—that handsome, dignified brother—died.

He was too capable, and even to the cruel Emperor, he was too cruel.

Since it was uncomfortable for the Emperor to see, he probably pinned a suitable crime on him and, as you said Evan, had his head cut off.

Without giving him time to speak, without letting him meet his family.

That's how the future of our family disappeared in an instant."

I muttered to myself.

And there's no one who could replace that man.

Me? Haha. Not a chance.

Unless all those half-wits and family elders were killed off...

"I wonder why Father died.

The news of Mother's promiscuity, whom he never cared about, spread around, and he must have thought he couldn't prevent the family's collapse.

And if he didn't kill himself, the Emperor would have arranged a disgraceful death for him.

Yes, it wasn't an accident; he must have jumped off a cliff somewhere. Or hanged himself."

Evan frowns and says in a somewhat growling voice:

"Erica, stop talking now."

But Evan's words didn't make much of an impression on me.

If I die, time rewinds a little bit.

Just a little.

Just a little.

But maybe I can repeat it infinitely.

If I die, that's actually good.

Because after the pain comes peace.

I'll just think of it as following Father.

Being killed by others was quite frightening and painful.

It might be just a feeling, but I feel like I might meet that awkward executioner again.
